headlines.title: Democrats’ deepening Israel divisions: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: In today’s edition, our Capitol Hill team dives into the latest disagreement among Democrats over Israel. Plus, Andrea Mitchell examines whether Ukraine can build on the advantages it has developed in the war with Russia.

headlines.title: ‘Democracy demands dialogue and debate’: Bipartisan duo address housing affordability
headlines.description: A landmark bipartisan housing bill became law last week, despite President Trump calling it “a big yawn” and canceling the signing ceremony. Rep. Mike Flood (R-Neb.) and Rep. Emanuel Cleaver (D-Mo.) join Meet the Press NOW as part of NBC’s Common Ground series to discuss their bipartisan effort to address housing affordability.

headlines.title: Blanche questioned on Epstein files, crime and IRS settlement in confirmation hearing
headlines.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche faced questions from senators on the handling of the Epstein files, crime statistics and a Trump settlement with the IRS during his confirmation hearing to permanently serve in the position. NBC News' Julie Tsirkin reports from Capitol Hill on what lawmakers highlighted in their questioning.

headlines.title: Epstein survivors in attendance for Todd Blanche's confirmation hearing
headlines.description: Survivors of Jeffrey Epstein are in attendance for acting Attorney General Todd Blanche's confirmation hearing to serve permanently in the role. NBC News' Kelly O'Donnell reports on their message to lawmakers and the questions Blanche has faced on the Epstein files.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: House Democrats split over U.S. aid to Israel as public opinion shifts
headlines.description: More than 100 Democrats voted to cut off U.S. assistance to Israel. The vote also sparked a rare division between the top two House Democrats: Hakeem Jeffries and Katherine Clark.

headlines.title: Indian billionaire denies U.S. investment pledge influenced DOJ to seek case dismissal
headlines.description: Indian business magnate Gautam Adani said his pledge to invest billions of dollars in the U.S. did not influence the Justice Department’s decision to move to drop the criminal case against him.

headlines.title: U.S. military will test service members’ testosterone levels, Pete Hegseth says
headlines.description: The U.S. military will begin testing the testosterone levels of some service members and recommending hormone therapy if needed, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Wednesday.

headlines.title: House passes Trump-backed bill that would make daylight saving time permanent
headlines.description: The legislation, known as the Sunshine Protection Act and backed by President Donald Trump, now heads to the Senate for consideration.

headlines.title: House Republicans push for $95B for Iran war, election measures and farm aid
headlines.description: House Republicans kicked off their plans for a third reconciliation bill for $90 billion in new money for the military during the Iran war and election-related measures.

headlines.title: Trump’s intelligence nominee hedges when asked about the 2020 election
headlines.description: Trump’s nominee for director of national intelligence, Jay Clayton, struggled to answer questions from Democratic lawmakers that could put him at odds with the president.

headlines.title: ICE and the Senate react to the latest shootings: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: Welcome to From the Politics Desk, a daily newsletter that brings you the NBC News Politics team’s latest reporting and analysis from the White House, Capitol Hill and the campaign trail.
